denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 03/31/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100530573          Group #: GRP-1067
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-113200
Claim #: CLM-2026-191552
Date of Service (requested): 03/11/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 03/13/2026 for Teprotumumab for thyroid eye disease related to Graves disease with ophthalmopathy (E05.00). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Teprotumumab for thyroid eye disease
Diagnosis (as submitted): Graves disease with ophthalmopathy (E05.00)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0127 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This determination is based on a plan exclusion. Your plan is a fully-insured group health plan subject to Washington State insurance regulations. State coverage mandates do not alter the terms of this plan for this determination.
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0127 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. The requested frequency exceeds plan guidelines for Graves disease with ophthalmopathy (E05.00). Additional visits are not medically necessary at this cadence.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director to review clinical information before you file a written appeal.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
If required information is not received within the applicable timeframe, the appeal may be administratively closed as withdrawn.
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100530573) and reference UM-2026-113200.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Graves disease with ophthalmopathy (E05.00). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Teprotumumab for thyroid eye disease recommended as the next appropriate step. CAS score 5/7 with diplopia; euthyroid on methimazole. The denial letter does not identify the name, specialty, or board certification of any reviewing clinician; the signatory is listed only as "Aetna Medical Management / Utilization Management Department" with no individual physician disclosed. The denial letter does not reference or address the submitted CAS score of 5/7, the documented diplopia, or the treating physician's methimazole records, none of which were acknowledged in the adverse determination.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
